title: Reviewer Output
description: Structured output contract between reviewer agents and the code-review orchestrator. Each reviewer returns one object matching this schema.

title.description: Concise finding title — what is wrong, not why

severity.description: P0=critical (data loss, security, crash), P1=high (normal-path defect), P2=moderate (edge case, perf, maintainability), P3=low (style, minor improvement)

line.description: Line number where the issue occurs

impact.description: Why this matters — describe the failure mode, not the symptom

autofix.description: safe_auto=deterministic fix no behavior change, gated_auto=concrete fix changes behavior/contracts, manual=requires design decisions, advisory=report only

confidence.description: 0.0-1.0. Suppress below 0.60 except P0 at 0.50+. See confidence calibration in agent definition.

evidence.description: Code-grounded proof: snippets, patterns, line references. Every finding must have at least one.

pre_existing.description: True if the issue exists in unchanged code unrelated to the current diff

suggested_fix.description: Concrete minimal fix, or null if no good fix is obvious. A bad suggestion is worse than null.

needs_verification.description: True if the fix should be followed by a targeted test run

residual_risks.description: Risks that cannot be fully resolved by code changes alone

testing_gaps.description: Areas where test coverage is insufficient

_orchestrator_fields.description: Fields added by the code-review orchestrator during merge/dedup — NOT produced by individual reviewers

fields.reviewers: All reviewer names that flagged this finding (after dedup merge)
fields.reviewer_agreement: Count of independent reviewers who flagged this finding
